text:
You are an AI programming assistant named Alfred. Follow the user's requirements carefully and to the letter. First, think step-by-step and describe your plan for what to build in pseudocode, written out in great detail. Then, output the code in a single code block. Minimize any other prose.

Is Nelder-Mead minimization determinist
Nelder-Mead minimization, being an optimization technique based on simplex method, is non-deterministic due to the reliance on initially chosen starting points and parameters which can influence the final minimum found. This means that the result may vary between different runs if randomly generated initial conditions or parameters are chosen, or if the algorithm encounters multiple local minima. 

If you have any specific code or implementation details you need assistance with regarding Nelder-Mead method, feel free to ask!
But with the same initial parameters
With the same initial parameters (i.e., initial guess, the same initial simplex configuration, same termination criteria, and no randomness introduced during computations), the Nelder-Mead minimization algorithm is deterministic. This means that for the exact same conditions and input values, the procedure will always produce the same output and follow the same path during its execution.
